back_translation: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Speak to Aaron and say to him: 'When you set up the lamps, toward the front face of the lampstand, the seven lamps shall give light.'"
And Aaron did so; toward the front face of the lampstand he set up its lamps, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
And this is the workmanship of the lampstand: hammered gold, from its base to its blossom, hammered it was; according to the pattern which the LORD showed Moses, so he made the lampstand.
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Take the Levites from among the sons of Israel and cleanse them.

And thus you shall do to them to cleanse them: sprinkle upon them the water of sin offering, and they shall pass a razor over all their flesh, and shall wash their garments, and shall purify themselves.
And they shall take a bull son of the herd, and its grain offering, fine flour mixed with oil; and a second bull son of the herd you shall take for a sin offering.
And you shall present the Levites before the Tent of Meeting, and you shall assemble the whole congregation of the sons of Israel.
And you shall present the Levites before the LORD, and the sons of Israel shall lay their hands upon the Levites.
And Aaron shall wave the Levites — a wave offering before the LORD — from the sons of Israel, so that they may perform the service of the LORD.
And the Levites shall lay their hands on the heads of the bulls; and you shall make one a sin offering and one a burnt offering to the LORD, to make atonement for the Levites.
And you shall have the Levites stand before Aaron and before his sons, and you shall wave them as a wave offering to the LORD.
And you shall separate the Levites from among the sons of Israel, and the Levites shall be Mine.
And after that the Levites shall come to do the work of the Tent of Meeting; and you shall cleanse them and wave them as a wave offering.
For they are wholly given to Me from among the sons of Israel; in place of every one who opens the womb, every firstborn from the sons of Israel, I have taken them for Myself.
For every firstborn among the sons of Israel is Mine, both man and beast; on the day I struck every firstborn in the land of Egypt I consecrated them for Myself.
And I have taken the Levites in place of every firstborn among the sons of Israel.

And I have given the Levites — given — to Aaron and to his sons from among the sons of Israel, to do the service of the sons of Israel in the Tent of Meeting and to make atonement for the sons of Israel; and there shall not be a plague upon the sons of Israel when they approach the sanctuary."
So Moses and Aaron and the whole congregation of the sons of Israel did with the Levites; according to all that the LORD commanded Moses regarding the Levites, so the sons of Israel did with them.
And the Levites purified themselves and washed their garments; and Aaron waved them as a wave offering before the LORD; and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them.
And after that the Levites came to do their service in the Tent of Meeting before Aaron and before his sons; as the LORD commanded Moses regarding the Levites, so they did to them.
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"This is what concerns the Levites: from a son of twenty-five years and upward he shall come to enter the service-unit in the work of the Tent of Meeting.
And from a son of fifty years he shall return from the service-unit of the work, and shall not serve any longer.
And he shall serve his brothers in the Tent of Meeting to keep the duty; but work he shall not do. Thus you shall do to the Levites in their duties."
